title: Thelypteris loheriana
intro: Thelypteris loheriana, also known as Loher's maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and ability to thrive in shaded, humid environments. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris loheriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Thelypteris loheriana is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Thelypteris loheriana is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This plant is a species of the genus Thelypteris, a genus of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Thelypteris loheriana is native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, where it grows in shaded areas and along streams. Its classification helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships and adaptations to its environment.
